TITLE: Visor 2030

HOMEPAGE.TITLE: Local SDG indicators in the province of Barcelona
HOMEPAGE.DESCRIPTION: From the Diputació de Barcelona, we offer a platform to visualize a system of multidimensional indicators aligned with the 2030 Agenda, reflecting the economic, social, and environmental reality of the municipalities in the province of Barcelona. With this dataset, we aim to encourage debate, reflection, and analysis, as well as to improve decision-making by both local authorities and the Diputació itself.
HOMEPAGE.SUBTITLE: About the SDGs
HOMEPAGE.SDG_DESCRIPTION: The Sustainable Development Goals are a global call to end poverty, protect the planet, and ensure well-being for all people. Adopted by the United Nations in 2015, they provide a common framework to address major global challenges. At the Diputació de Barcelona, we promote their local implementation as a tool for planning, making informed decisions, and monitoring progress towards a more sustainable and fair future.
HOMEPAGE.FOOTER_TEXT:
Visor 2030 is an open-source platform that brings together a comprehensive set of multidimensional indicators reflecting the social, economic, and environmental realities of municipalities across the province of Barcelona, aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). These indicators were chosen based on their thematic relevance and the availability of municipal-level data.

The source code, data architecture, and full set of indicators are openly available for other public institutions to reuse, adapt, and improve.

Visor 2030 supports local decision-making, facilitates monitoring of the Agenda 2030 across the territory, and provides a holistic view of SDG progress within the demarcation of Barcelona.

NO_MUNICIPALITY_DATA: No data available for this municipality
NO_COMARCA_DATA: No data available for this county
NO_AGGREGATION_DATA: No data available for this aggregation
MUN_NOT_SEA: This municipality has no coastal area

AGRUPACIO_NOT_SEA: This aggregation has no coastal area
CHOOSE_TARGET: Choose the target you want to see indicators for
CLICK_SDG: Click on any of the SDGs to see detailed information
ERROR: There was an error loading the data. Please try again later.
BUDGET_SUBTITLE: The allocated budget reflects the financial resources dedicated to initiatives linked to the SDGs. It shows both the total and the distribution by goals, as well as the unassigned portion. This analysis helps evaluate the alignment between local policies and sustainability commitments. These are budgeted and assigned resources but not yet settled.

METODOLOGIA_SHORT_DESC:
- The process of localizing the SDGs and selecting targets applicable at the municipal level.
- The criteria used to select the indicators.
- The adjustments applied: weighting by population, segmentations, aggregations, winsorization.
- The calculation of the synthetic index and its interpretation.
- The territorial and temporal coverage, and the data update model.
METODOLOGIA_DESC:
The Visor 2030 aims to measure and compare the level of achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in the municipalities of the Province of Barcelona using weighted indicators and a synthetic index.

The methodological procedure is detailed below:

METODOLOGIA_PAGE.LOCALITZACIO_DESC: A localization process was carried out to identify the targets most relevant in the municipal context. This process made it possible to prioritize targets related to areas where local governments have direct action capacity and where statistical data is available.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.SELECCIO_INDICADORS_DESC: A total of 76 indicators are shown, distributed across the 17 SDGs, covering social, economic, environmental, and institutional dimensions. Efforts were made to ensure a balanced distribution among goals and adequate territorial representation.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.SELECCIO_INDICADORS_TEXT:
**The selection of indicators was carried out according to the following criteria:**

- Availability of data for a representative number of municipalities in the province.
- Existence of historical data with the highest possible temporal continuity, depending on each source's availability.
- Relevance in relation to the reference target.
- Source quality (mainly official and public sources).

**The selection of indicators is based on the following references:**

- United Nations Statistical Commission, in relation to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.
- SDG indicator set for the European Union, led by Eurostat.
- Sustainable Development Goals indicators for Catalunya, developed by Idescat together with CADS (Advisory Council for Sustainable Development of Catalunya).
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.PONDERACIO_DESC: To ensure comparability between municipalities with very diverse characteristics, several methodological adjustments have been applied to the indicators:
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.PONDERACIO_1_DESC: In indicators where absolute volume could introduce biases, values have been adjusted by considering the number of inhabitants in each municipality. This makes it possible to obtain relative measures (per person or per thousand inhabitants), which are more comparable between municipalities of different sizes.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.PONDERACIO_2_DESC: In some cases, percentage calculations, segmentations by groups (for example, by sex or age groups), as well as aggregations of related indicators, have been applied.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.PONDERACIO_3_TITLE: Territorial comparison with the county and the province
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.PONDERACIO_3_DESC: To help contextualize municipal results, county and provincial reference values have been included. In cases where the data source does not provide directly aggregated county or provincial information, statistical averages have been calculated from the available municipal values within each territorial scope.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.PONDERACIO_4_DESC: To prevent outliers from distorting the results of the indicators and the synthetic index, a winsorization process has been applied. This method involves limiting the most extreme values of an indicator by replacing them with threshold percentiles. In this way, the impact of extreme cases is reduced without eliminating data, ensuring comparability.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.INDEX_SINTETIC_DESC:
The synthetic SDG achievement index for each municipality is built from a specific selection of indicators for each SDG, chosen based on their thematic relevance and the availability of data with municipal coverage.

Each indicator is assigned a specific weight within its corresponding goal, according to technical criteria established to ensure a balance between representativeness and statistical quality. The weighted values are then aggregated to calculate an overall municipal index, expressed on a scale from 0 to 100, where 100 represents the highest level of SDG achievement across all considered indicators.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.INDEX_SINTETIC_FORMULA:
**General formula of the index**

- Positive indicator  = ((Value - Min) / (Max - Min) * 100) * weight
- Negative indicator = ((Max - Value) / (Max - Min) * 100) * weight
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.COBERTURA_DESC: The viewer includes municipalities in the Province of Barcelona that have sufficient data to calculate the index. The analyzed period adapts to the data availability of each source and may vary depending on the indicator.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.ACTUALITZACIO_DESC: The methodology is designed to be scalable and adaptable. The progressive incorporation of new indicators and targets is planned, as well as the periodic update of data. All future methodological changes will be documented and published.
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.NOTA_DESC: In some specific cases, the most recent data may contain anomalous values or may not be available for certain municipalities, due to changes in the original sources or issues in the automatic update processes. In these cases, only the years and values considered reliable are shown
METODOLOGIA_PAGE.AGRUPACIONS_TERRITORIALS_DESC:
The Visor 2030 allows indicators to be analysed across different territorial levels and criteria. Data can be explored by county, section, municipality and other aggregations that categorise municipalities according to shared characteristics.

This approach facilitates comparisons between municipalities, the identification of territorial patterns, and a more contextualised interpretation of results.
The definition and full description of all available aggregations can be consulted through the information selector located on the right-hand side.

rur.title: Rural municipalities
rur.description: Municipalities defined by Law 8/2025 of 30 July on the Statute of Rural Municipalities. A rural municipality has a population equal to or below 2,000 inhabitants and belongs to a rural county. It also includes municipalities with fewer than 2,000 inhabitants belonging to neighbouring counties that meet one of the following conditions: population density equal to or below 90 inhabitants per square kilometre or negative population growth over the last ten years.

ABOUT_PAGE.QUE_ES_DESC:
Visor 2030 is an initiative of the **Diputació de Barcelona** created to provide data on the **social, economic, and environmental reality of the municipalities in the province of Barcelona**, framed through the SDGs.

Visor 2030 is not just a database. It is a living knowledge space that offers municipalities **localized, updated, and SDG-aligned information**, so they can design, implement, and evaluate public policies with a transformative vision.
ABOUT_PAGE.OBJECTIUS_DESC:
- Facilitate the localization, alignment, and monitoring of the 2030 Agenda and the SDGs in the Barcelona area.
- Guide decision-making by providing useful information for local planning and policy development processes.
- Help raise awareness of the SDGs and their local implementation among citizens.
- Offer a 360º view with different types of data on SDG progress in the territory.
ABOUT_PAGE.A_QUI_DESC:
- **Local governments**: a decision-making compass for politicians and technical staff, providing a quick snapshot of the municipality and the territory as a whole to identify priority areas for action.
- **Citizens**: intuitive visualizations and simplified reports that help explain the SDGs and engage society in shared challenges.
- **Diputació de Barcelona**: support for technical teams in the territory by providing contextual data from multidimensional indicators and territorial analyses.
- **Data experts**: analytical flexibility with advanced filters and the option to download raw data for customized studies.
ABOUT_PAGE.QUI_SOM_DESC:
The project is led by the **Strategy 2030 Service** with support from the **Technical Office for Economic Development Strategies** and the **Technical Internet Office** of the Diputació de Barcelona.

The development of the indicator set and the ongoing monitoring and improvement of the platform are carried out in collaboration with a **group of expert data advisors** from various areas of the Diputació de Barcelona.
ABOUT_PAGE.SOLUCIO_DESC:
Visor 2030 is a **flexible tool**. Designed to grow and adapt. With a modular and integrative approach that allows for the addition of new indicators and functionalities, **evolving** as needs change.

Moreover, it is an **open-source** project. Its source code, architecture, and indicator logic are available so that **other public institutions can reuse, adapt, and improve them**, building alliances to provide local governments with high-quality data to advance effective sustainable development.

**For more information**: Strategy 2030 Service ([s.agenda2030{'@'}diba.cat](mailto:s.agenda2030{'@'}diba.cat))
